skill.name: amazon-reviews-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users automatically extract Amazon product reviews via the Amazon Reviews API. Agent should proactively apply this skill when users express needs like getting reviews for Amazon product with ASIN B07TS6R1SF, analyzing customer feedback for a specific Amazon item, getting ratings and comments for a competitive product, tracking sentiment of recent Amazon reviews, extracting verified purchase reviews for quality assessment, summarizing user experiences from Amazon product pages, monitoring product performance through customer reviews, collecting reviewer profiles and links for market research, gathering review titles and descriptions for content analysis, scraping Amazon reviews without requiring a login.
skill.summary: Extract Amazon product reviews via the BrowserAct API by providing an ASIN.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis identified network access, environment variable usage, and script execution patterns. After evaluation, these are all legitimate patterns for a data extraction skill. The Python script properly retrieves API credentials from environment variables rather than hardcoding them. Network calls are to the expected BrowserAct API endpoint. The critical heuristic finding is a false positive triggered by the combination of env_access and network patterns, which are appropriate for this skill type.

medium_findings.title: External Command Documentation in SKILL.md
medium_findings.description: The SKILL.md contains backtick syntax examples showing script invocation commands. These are documentation examples in code blocks, not actual command execution risks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick patterns in SKILL.md are within code block fences showing example commands. No actual command injection vectors found in the skill files.
medium_findings.reasoning: The backtick syntax is standard markdown for code examples. The skill instructs Claude to execute a specific Python script with a single ASIN argument, which is the intended use case.

low_findings.title: Network Access to BrowserAct API
low_findings.description: Python script makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls are to hardcoded BrowserAct API endpoint for legitimate review extraction purpose.
low_findings.reasoning: Network access is expected for this skill type. The hardcoded URL points to the BrowserAct service, not external third-party services. Data flows to the user through the API response.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: Script retrieves BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Secure pattern - API key is retrieved from environment, never hardcoded in source code.
low_findings.reasoning: Using os.getenv() to retrieve API credentials from environment is a security best practice, not a vulnerability. The static analyzer incorrectly flagged this as 'Generic API/secret keys' access. This pattern prevents credentials from being stored in source code.

low_findings.title: API Response Parsing
low_findings.description: Script checks for task IDs and error messages in API responses.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Normal API response handling patterns, not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.reasoning: Checking for 'id' in response and error messages like 'Invalid authorization' is standard API response handling. The static analyzer misclassified this as 'System reconnaissance'.

content.user_title: Extract Amazon Product Reviews via API
content.value_statement: Need to gather customer reviews from Amazon for analysis? This skill automates review extraction using the BrowserAct API, giving you structured data without manual scraping or login requirements.
actual_capabilities: Extract structured reviews from Amazon by ASIN
Retrieve reviewer names, ratings, and review text
Access verified purchase status and review dates
Monitor review sentiment through timestamped data
Collect reviewer profile links for follow-up
Handle review variants and country information
limitations: Requires valid BrowserAct API key (BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable)
Execution time depends on task queue; may take several minutes for completion
Data scope limited to reviews accessible via BrowserAct workflow template

use_cases.title: Market Research and Competitor Analysis
use_cases.description: Extract reviews from competitor products to understand customer pain points and identify market opportunities.

use_cases.title: Quality Assurance Monitoring
use_cases.description: Collect customer feedback to identify product issues and track quality trends over time.

use_cases.title: Sentiment Analysis Training Data
use_cases.description: Gather review text and ratings to build or improve sentiment analysis models.

prompt_templates.title: Extract reviews for a product
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the amazon-reviews-api-skill to get the reviews for ASIN B07TS6R1SF.

prompt_templates.title: Compare reviews across products
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract reviews for ASIN B07TS6R1SF and ASIN B08N5WRWJ6 using the amazon-reviews-api-skill. Then summarize the key differences in customer satisfaction.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor recent feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the amazon-reviews-api-skill to get the latest 50 reviews for ASIN B07TS6R1SF. Identify any emerging complaints or praise patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Verify purchase analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract reviews from ASIN B07TS6R1SF using the amazon-reviews-api-skill. Separate verified vs non-verified purchase reviews and calculate the average rating for each group.

output_examples.input: Compare reviews between two similar products
output_examples.output:
Review Comparison:

Product A (B07TS6R1SF): 4.5 avg, 85% verified
Product B (B08N5WRWJ6): 3.8 avg, 60% verified

Winner: Product A has higher satisfaction and more verified buyers.

Main Product A advantage: Quality consistency
Main Product B advantage: Lower price point

Recommendation: Product A preferred for quality; Product B for budget users.

best_practices: Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before invoking the skill to avoid authorization errors.
Monitor the task status output during execution; the script polls for completion and may take several minutes.
Check for 'Invalid authorization' in output first when troubleshooting errors, as this indicates an expired or invalid API key.
anti_patterns: Do not invoke the script without providing an ASIN argument, as this causes immediate script exit.
Do not ignore error messages about 'Invalid authorization' - recheck your API key configuration.
Do not exceed the single automatic retry limit; multiple retries indicate a configuration issue that needs resolution.

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key set as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable. Get your key from browseract.com/reception/integrations.

faq.question: How long does it take to extract reviews?
faq.answer: Task execution varies based on queue load. The script polls for up to 5 minutes and reports status every 10 seconds.

faq.question: What information is included in each review?
faq.answer: Extracted data includes reviewer name, profile link, star rating, review title, review text, publication date, country, product variant, and verified purchase status.

faq.question: Can I extract reviews from any Amazon product?
faq.answer: You need the ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number) for the product. Any valid Amazon product ASIN should work with the BrowserAct workflow.

faq.question: Why did the extraction fail?
faq.answer: Common causes: invalid API key (look for 'Invalid authorization' in output), network issues, or task timeout. Retry once automatically if not an auth error.

faq.question: How many reviews can I extract per request?
faq.answer: The BrowserAct workflow processes reviews based on its template configuration. For large volumes, consider making multiple requests with different time windows.
